product.name: YNX Data Fabric
product.title: YNX Data Fabric — Verifiable events, billing, and reconciliation
product.metaDescription: The shared YNX Testnet data plane for signed events, recoverable cross-product workflows, immutable billing records, and source-labeled reconciliation.
product.h1: Verifiable events. Recoverable workflows. Balanced books.

localizedNames.ja: YNX データファブリック

features: Strict signed event envelopes with product, actor, ordering, source, privacy, retention, and audit authority
Transactional Outbox and consumer Inbox with idempotent effects, retry, dead letter, replay, and backfill
Recoverable cross-product Sagas with compensation, timeout, audit, and user-visible status
Append-only double-entry billing records and source-labeled reconciliation
risks: An accepted HTTP request does not mean a multi-service business workflow is complete.
Testnet evidence does not establish Mainnet, production signing, availability, capacity, or financial suitability.
Third-party and derived observations can be unavailable or inconsistent and never replace authoritative product or ledger state.
Operator recovery and reconciliation may require human review; historical financial records are corrected with new entries, never silently edited.

faq.question: Is YNX Data Fabric a wallet or custody service?
faq.answer: No. Products and the canonical Wallet remain authoritative for identity, permissions, assets, and custody. Data Fabric transports signed facts and records workflow, billing, and reconciliation state.

faq.question: Does it guarantee exactly-once network delivery?
faq.answer: No. Delivery can repeat. Transactional Outbox and Inbox records plus idempotent state transitions provide exactly-once effects within each controlled state boundary.

faq.question: Can analytics change a bill or settlement?
faq.answer: No. Analytics is derived and cannot modify the authoritative operational event store or immutable Billing Ledger.

faq.question: Is it publicly released?
faq.answer: No. Local component tests exist, but central integration, staging and public deployment, hosted downloads, production signing, and store release are not evidenced.
